message:
fix(fr-runtime+fr-command+fr-conformance): CLIENT error wording + INFO/ID canonicalizer (br-frankenredis-w579)

Wording fixes to match upstream networking.c:
  - CLIENT NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH invalid mode → plain SyntaxError
    (was "ERR argument must be 'on' or 'off'")
  - CLIENT SETINFO LIB-NAME/LIB-VER with space → "ERR LIB-NAME
    cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters."
    (was "lib-name can only contain characters that are allowed in
    CLIENT SETNAME")
  - CLIENT PAUSE negative timeout → "ERR timeout is negative"
    (was silently accepted, returning OK)
  - CLIENT CACHING YES/NO with tracking disabled or wrong mode →
    the mode-specific "YES is only valid when tracking is enabled
    in OPTIN mode" / "NO is only valid when tracking is enabled in
    OPTOUT mode" (was the generic "CLIENT CACHING can be called
    only when..." reply)

Harness canonicalizers for volatile per-connection fields:
  - CLIENT ID accepts any positive integer (id counter differs
    between upstream Python client and our single-client harness).
  - HELLO accepts the reply with any version string / positive id.
  - CLIENT INFO projects to the behavioral subset (name, db, user,
    cmd, lib-name, lib-ver, sub, psub, ssub, multi, resp); addr,
    laddr, fd, age, idle, qbuf, rbs, rbp, tot-mem, argv-mem,
    multi-mem, redir, flags are environmental.

Updated fixture expectations in core_client.json. Updated 3
fr-command unit tests to assert the new upstream-aligned replies.

Live oracle: core_client now 124/125 pass (was 113/125); the one
remaining divergence is an OBJECT IDLETIME wall-clock-vs-fixture
drift that falls under the shared TTL-accounting gap tracked in
br-frankenredis-7rp6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
